content:
- Deep-verified and then executed story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/01: added backup-provider abstraction + pgBackRest integration wiring.
- Implemented `src/backup/*` (typed ops + pgBackRest renderer + runtime-facing job builders) and process-worker integration (new job kinds/specs + build_command + timeout mapping + unit tests + real-binary `pgbackrest --version` smoke).
- Extended config v2 schema with `[backup]` block, added `process.backup_timeout_ms`, added `process.binaries.pgbackrest` as optional (required when `backup.enabled=true`), and added `BinaryPathsV2Input` so missing fields stay actionable `ConfigError::Validation.field` instead of serde parse errors.
- Updated harness/provenance: added `expected_version.kind=pgbackrest` (major check), updated remediation strings, added pgBackRest to real-binaries policy, and added hardened `tools/install-pgbackrest.sh`.
- Installer gotcha: AlmaLinux aarch64 lacked `pgbackrest` in base repos and PGDG package depended on `libssh2` (not available until enabling EPEL). Enabled EPEL repo and used PGDG repo RPM bootstrap path; script updated to auto-enable `epel`.
- Updated docs (operator config + quick-start + contributor testing/harness docs) to document pgBackRest and `[backup]` config semantics.
- Gates all green: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`.
